name: element_not_at_index
code:
proof fn phi_1_element_not_at_index<K: KeyTrait + VerusClone>(
    old_seq: Seq<K>, new_seq: Seq<K>, k: K, i: int
)
    requires
        sorted(old_seq),
        old_seq.no_duplicates(),
        !old_seq.contains(k),
        new_seq.len() == old_seq.len() + 1,
        0 <= i < new_seq.len(),
        new_seq == old_seq.insert(i, k),
        new_seq.to_set() == old_seq.to_set().insert(k),
    ensures
        new_seq[i] != k,
{
}
reason: If the element at the returned insertion index is not k, the spec fails to place k correctly

name: always_appended_at_end
code:
proof fn phi_2_always_appended_at_end<K: KeyTrait + VerusClone>(
    old_seq: Seq<K>, new_seq: Seq<K>, k: K, i: int
)
    requires
        sorted(old_seq),
        old_seq.no_duplicates(),
        !old_seq.contains(k),
        old_seq.len() > 0,
        new_seq.len() == old_seq.len() + 1,
        0 <= i < new_seq.len(),
        new_seq == old_seq.insert(i, k),
    ensures
        i == old_seq.len(),
{
}
reason: If the insertion index is always the last position, sorting is not actually constraining placement

name: old_element_lost
code:
proof fn phi_3_old_element_lost<K: KeyTrait + VerusClone>(
    old_seq: Seq<K>, new_seq: Seq<K>, k: K, i: int
)
    requires
        sorted(old_seq),
        old_seq.no_duplicates(),
        !old_seq.contains(k),
        old_seq.len() > 0,
        new_seq.len() == old_seq.len() + 1,
        0 <= i < new_seq.len(),
        new_seq == old_seq.insert(i, k),
        new_seq.to_set() == old_seq.to_set().insert(k),
    ensures
        !new_seq.contains(old_seq[0]),
{
}
reason: If a pre-existing element disappears after insert, the spec fails to preserve old contents

name: set_unchanged_after_insert
code:
proof fn phi_4_set_unchanged_after_insert<K: KeyTrait + VerusClone>(
    old_seq: Seq<K>, new_seq: Seq<K>, k: K, i: int
)
    requires
        sorted(old_seq),
        old_seq.no_duplicates(),
        !old_seq.contains(k),
        new_seq.len() == old_seq.len() + 1,
        0 <= i < new_seq.len(),
        new_seq == old_seq.insert(i, k),
        new_seq.to_set() == old_seq.to_set().insert(k),
    ensures
        new_seq.to_set() =~= old_seq.to_set(),
{
}
reason: If the set view is unchanged, k was never actually added — the spec's set postcondition is vacuous

name: first_element_always_changes
code:
proof fn phi_5_first_element_always_changes<K: KeyTrait + VerusClone>(
    old_seq: Seq<K>, new_seq: Seq<K>, k: K, i: int
)
    requires
        sorted(old_seq),
        old_seq.no_duplicates(),
        !old_seq.contains(k),
        old_seq.len() >= 1,
        new_seq.len() == old_seq.len() + 1,
        0 <= i < new_seq.len(),
        new_seq == old_seq.insert(i, k),
    ensures
        new_seq[0] != old_seq[0],
{
}
reason: If the first element always changes, insertion incorrectly disrupts prefix stability when k is not minimal
